# AGENTS.md

## Purpose and scope

This is the canonical shared repo instruction layer for `Dev-portfolio`.
`CLAUDE.md` in this repo should import this file. Update the source notes in the Obsidian second-brain and republish instead of editing this file directly.

## Project summary

Next.js marketing site for NexGen Studio with a lead capture flow that validates requests and forwards them to an n8n webhook.

## Stack and entrypoints

- Next.js App Router
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- Framer Motion
- Zod validation
- n8n webhook integration

Primary entrypoints:
- src/app/page.tsx for the main landing page composition
- src/app/api/contact/route.ts for lead capture validation and webhook forwarding
- src/components for section-level UI and reusable layout primitives

## Working expectations

- Keep changes scoped to the task at hand.
- Prefer small, reviewable edits tied to a concrete objective.
- Follow the repo's architecture boundaries before expanding a refactor.
- Run the smallest relevant validation before wrapping up.
- If workflow or command guidance changes, update the source notes and republish rather than editing this file by hand.

## Validation commands

- npm run lint
- npm run build

## Repo guardrails

- Keep the marketing narrative and CTA flow coherent across sections.
- Do not weaken spam protection, server-side validation, or webhook authentication when editing forms.
- Avoid broad component churn unless the task requires a coordinated landing-page rewrite.

## Docs map

- README.md: setup, env variables, CRO flags, and local run instructions
- docs/N8N_WORKFLOW_SETUP.md: n8n integration setup
- docs/OPERATIONS.md: deployment and operations notes

## Domain terminology

- CRO mode: feature flags that control advanced conversion-rate behaviors
- Intent matching: query-driven hero and copy variants
- Lead capture: the contact form flow that forwards validated requests to n8n

## Selected rule packs

### Frontend Marketing Sites

- Optimize for clear messaging, strong mobile behavior, and conversion-focused UX.
- Preserve the established visual language of the repo; if the task requires a redesign, define a specific visual direction instead of defaulting to generic Tailwind patterns.
- Keep form validation mirrored on both client and server when a lead form exists.
- For meaningful UI work, prefer validating with both lint and build rather than only one check.

## Repo-specific notes

This repo is a business-facing landing page, so copy quality and conversion flow matter as much as code structure. Preserve existing section rhythm unless a task explicitly calls for a redesign.
